_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: Three storylines define the 2026 Big 12: Texas Tech is the clear favorite this year after winning the Big 12 title game last year and making the College Football playoff, but lost to Oregon due to a limited offense; Oklahoma State is expected to bounce back from a dismal year with a new head coach Eric Morris and many of his North Texas players transferred with him; two of the top players expected in the Big 12 will likely miss the season for legal reasons, Texas Tech QB Brendan Sorsby, a portal addition from Cincinnati entered gambling rehab and will likely be suspended by the NCAA & BYU WR Parker Kingston for much more serious charges.  The conference as a whole is very volatile, Calibration on volatility: the Big 12 has the loosest hierarchy in the P4. Multiple different teams have won the league in recent years and the preseason favorite has missed the title game more often than not. When team_notes give a 3-tier range, that's typical for this league — not a hedge. Frame the wide projections as the story: this is a league where the August #8 finishes 2nd in December more often than anywhere else. Resist flattening 3-tier ranges into a single outcome; the uncertainty IS the projection.
